title: DiCUNO G4 LED Bulbs 2W Warm White 3000K, 20W Halogen Equivalent, 12V, G4 Bi-Pin LED Light Bulbs, Non-Dimmable Light for Home Lighting, 200LM, Pack of 10
features: Bright Light, Energy-saving: DiCUNO G4 LED Bulbs give out 3000K warm white and 200LM brightness from 360° beam angle, offering a bright and energetic ambiance without bluish light. DiCUNO G4 2W LED Bulbs can replace traditional G4 20W halogen lamps with lower energy consumption, saving up to 90% on your electricity bill.

LED Transformer Required & Non-dimmable: An LED-compatible transformer is required for the G4 Capsule light bulb with AC/DC 12V low voltage. Please do not use with a dimmer. Flickering or shut-off might occur if a dimmer is used. Note: If minor flickering occurs, adding 1–3 halogen bulbs can help stabilize the circuit by meeting its minimum load requirement.

Distinct Advantages: The G4 LED lamps adopt high-quality clear silicone material, whose excellent heat dissipation with high light transmittance, is safer and more durable. CRI>85 restores the original colors. Flicker-free bulbs and stable lighting performance can avoid eye fatigue.

Mini Size, Capsule Bulb: The size of DiCUNO G4 Bi-Pin LED Bulb is only 10.2*37MM, whose high compatibility including standard G4 Bi-Pin base can fit most light fittings with G4 base. Easy and fast installation.
Wide Applications: G4 LED Bulbs are ideal for ceiling lamps, wall lamps, table lamps, bedside lamps, lawn lamps, cabinet lamps, range hood lamps, post lamps, etc.
description: Product Description DiCUNO G4 LED Bulbs--Energy-saving and Eye-protection 12V Clear Capsule G4 bi-pin light bulbs, 2W (20W equivalent), Warm White 3000K, CRI 85. Immerse Yourself in the Charm of Non-flickering Light! Previous page Next page G4 light bulbs--Browse to Learn More→ Previous page Next page Q1: Why does the bulb flicker or turn off after a few minutes? A1: Our bulb requires a stable 12V power supply. Please make sure to use an LED-compatible transformer. Using a traditional transformer designed for halogen bulbs may cause unstable voltage output, resulting in flickering or automatic shut-off. Q2: How can I fix the flickering or shut-off issue? A2: Use an LED-compatible transformer to ensure stable operation. If you cannot replace the transformer immediately, adding 1–3 halogen bulbs in the same circuit can help stabilize the voltage and reduce flickering. Q3: Can I use this bulb with a dimmer switch? A3: No. This bulb is non-dimmable.
